intro: Cordyceps sinensis, commonly known as Cordyceps or Cordyceps hookeriae, is a type of fungus that belongs to the family Cordycipitaceae. This medicinal mushroom has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fatigue, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as enhancing immunity, reducing oxidative stress, and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Cordyceps sinensis include cordycepin, polysaccharides, and peptide fractions, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cordyceps sinensis is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared as a powder, capsule, or tea, as well as being incorporated into various herbal preparations such as tinctures, extracts, and soups.
